caveats: a: The occupation mix covers several fabricated-metal subsectors and is not six-digit specific.
a: The frozen compensation ratio combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts and includes a harmonization adjustment.
rho: Representative five-year implementation evidence for LMM fluid-power suppliers was not found.
rho: Digital readiness varies sharply between modern CNC plants and legacy job shops.
e: The assigned 97-firm count is margin-bridged rather than an observed EBITDA-band census.
e: Establishments and firms differ because one operator may own multiple plants.
s5: No owner-age or succession panel specific to fluid-power manufacturers was found.
s5: Asset sales and distressed liquidations must be excluded unless a going concern changes control.
q: No representative contract dataset was available.
q: Retention differs between proprietary engineered valves, catalog fittings, and customer-designed build-to-print parts.
d5: Broad fabricated-metal output is not a forecast for NAICS 332912.
d5: Electrification may eliminate some hydraulic circuits while automation can increase pneumatic and specialized valve content.
o: This measures operator requirement for remaining component demand, not continued sourcing from the same independent supplier.
o: Customer insourcing changes the operator rather than necessarily eliminating manufacturing labor.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat component sales through OEM supply agreements, distributor replenishment, standing purchase orders, and replacement demand, typically priced per part, assembly, or lot rather than per labor hour.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal plants, distributors without manufacturing, field-only hose service firms classified elsewhere, commodity resellers, shell entities, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.included_activities: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of fluid-power valves, hose fittings, couplings, and related engineered components supplied repeatedly to external OEM, distributor, repair, and industrial customers.

a.bridge: BLS reports the broader fabricated-metal group containing 3329 with 6.64% management, 3.08% sales, and 9.16% office and administrative support employment, while the remainder includes substantial production and technical work; O*NET describes machinists as setting up and operating precision machine tools. I infer exposure in office, quoting, planning, procurement, customer-service, quality-document, and engineering-support tasks while excluding machining, assembly, testing, maintenance, material movement, and accountable inspection. The broad group may misstate the mix of engineering and automated production in fluid-power components.
caveats: The occupation mix covers several fabricated-metal subsectors and is not six-digit specific.
The frozen compensation ratio combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts and includes a harmonization adjustment.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task census from 20-30 independent fluid-power component manufacturers, including quoting, engineering, office, production, quality, maintenance, and logistics roles, would materially change the range.
a.rationale: AI can assist RFQ and drawing intake, quote preparation, production planning, procurement, customer communications, work-instruction drafting, quality-document search, and exception analysis. Core precision forming, machining, assembly, pressure testing, inspection, maintenance, and handling remain physical.

caveats: Broad fabricated-metal output is not a forecast for NAICS 332912.
Electrification may eliminate some hydraulic circuits while automation can increase pneumatic and specialized valve content.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year constant-price shipment forecasts by mobile hydraulic, industrial hydraulic, and pneumatic end market, reconciled to OEM build plans and distributor sell-through, would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Demand is diversified across mobile equipment, industrial automation, construction, agriculture, energy, transport, and replacement markets, supporting broadly stable real quantity. Cyclic equipment investment, electrification, leakage reduction, imports, and customer platform changes create downside; BLS reports broader fabricated-metal output fell 4.0% in 2024 after falling 5.0% in 2023, showing cyclicality rather than a reliable five-year trend.

caveats: The assigned 97-firm count is margin-bridged rather than an observed EBITDA-band census.
Establishments and firms differ because one operator may own multiple plants.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level census connecting SUSB-band manufacturers to ownership, external-customer revenue, recurring order share, product mix, and normalized EBITDA would materially change eligibility.
e.rationale: Independent manufacturers normally supply repeat external OEM, distributor, and replacement customers, so the recurring-customer lens fits most operating firms. Captive plants, resellers, firms dominated by nonrecurring projects, and companies outside the EBITDA band reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 332912 ($3,256,561,517) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 332912 ($11,302,317,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $372,620 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 332912); class <5: 59 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,299,831 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $255,027 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 34 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,783,147 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $349,853 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 40 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,123,025 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $808,938 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 61 firms, avg revenue/firm $11,857,098 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,326,363 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 36 firms, avg revenue/firm $39,233,972 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,697,705 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 37 firms, avg revenue/firm $239,577,649 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $47,005,135 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 97

caveats: This measures operator requirement for remaining component demand, not continued sourcing from the same independent supplier.
Customer insourcing changes the operator rather than necessarily eliminating manufacturing labor.
o.change_evidence: OEM platform roadmaps quantifying hydraulic-to-electric actuator substitution, additive manufacturing, customer insourcing, and elimination of hoses or valves would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Remaining demand for pressure-bearing valves, hoses, and fittings requires accountable physical manufacture, dimensional control, assembly, and testing. Software can improve selection and design but cannot replace the component; electrification or redesign can eliminate some hydraulic content from the current basket.

caveats: No representative contract dataset was available.
Retention differs between proprietary engineered valves, catalog fittings, and customer-designed build-to-print parts.
q.change_evidence: A representative sample of OEM and distributor agreements showing annual cost-downs, open-book terms, material indexation, rebates, tooling ownership, and renewal outcomes would materially change retention.
q.rationale: Per-part pricing and qualification or tooling friction allow savings to persist between negotiations. OEM annual cost-downs, distributor bargaining, indexed material surcharges, competitive rebids, and transparent should-cost analysis should pass a meaningful share to customers over five years.

caveats: Representative five-year implementation evidence for LMM fluid-power suppliers was not found.
Digital readiness varies sharply between modern CNC plants and legacy job shops.
rho.change_evidence: Before-and-after labor hours, quote cycle times, schedule adherence, scrap, and quality escapes from multi-site component makers using AI-enabled quoting, planning, document control, and inspection analytics would materially move the estimate.
rho.rationale: Common ERP, CAD, CRM, and document workflows make supervised assistants feasible within five years. Fragmented drawings and routings, customer-specific specifications, cybersecurity, low-volume variation, and the need for human engineering and quality approval constrain implementation, but regulation is lighter than in life-science manufacturing.

caveats: No owner-age or succession panel specific to fluid-power manufacturers was found.
Asset sales and distressed liquidations must be excluded unless a going concern changes control.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, Capital IQ, UCC ownership changes, and interviews with industrial M&A brokers reconciled to the eligible firm list would materially change the range.
s5.rationale: Customer qualifications, drawings, tooling, skilled teams, and replacement relationships are transferable and attractive to strategic component groups. No observed five-year transfer denominator for eligible firms was found, and facility or product-line purchases are not necessarily control transfers.

principal_driver: Recurring, safety-relevant physical components with repeat OEM and replacement demand.
principal_weakness: Most wage-bearing work is embodied production, while OEM procurement can reclaim a substantial share of realized savings.

run_meta.title: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing

sources.supports: Reports 470,070 jobs in the broader group containing 3329, with management at 6.64%, sales at 3.08%, and office and administrative support at 9.16% of employment.
sources.title: BLS May 2023 OEWS: Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

sources.supports: Defines machinists as setting up and operating machine tools to produce precision metal parts using knowledge of mechanics, mathematics, metal properties, layout, and machining procedures.
sources.title: O*NET: Machinists

sources.supports: Reports broader-sector output changes of -5.0% in 2023 and -4.0% in 2024, illustrating recent cyclicality rather than a six-digit forecast.
sources.title: BLS Industries at a Glance: Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing

sources.supports: Requires inspection of hydraulic and pneumatic hoses, fittings, tubing, pumps, motors, and valves for leaks, deformation, abrasion, pressure, and operational deficiencies, supporting safety-critical physical maintenance and replacement.
sources.title: OSHA 1926.1412 Inspections

sources.supports: Requires hoses, pipes, valves, filters, and fittings to remain within manufacturer safe operating pressures and mandates safety devices for certain hoses.
sources.title: OSHA 1926.302 Power-operated hand tools

title: Fluid Power Valve and Hose Fitting Manufacturing
